summary: Integrated agricultural calendar from Leymebamba — a multi-purpose reference document combining crop management, territorial administration, and seasonal timing. 769 cords (damaged — primary cord decomposed), 12 colors, deep tree to L5 (unique depth in corpus). Reclassified from astronomical to AGRICULTURAL CALENDAR: 12/13 words fit farming vocabulary (qaqa x7 = rocky terrain/terraces, kaki = corn/Pleiades, waki = communal labor, llaqa = village). All 21 STRING on L2/L3 (none on L1) = numbers are primary, words are annotations. Same site as UR006 (June 1473). 22 STRING, 95% coverage.
interpretation:
IDENTIFICATION
  769 cords (L1: 281, L2: 400, L3: 79, L4: 8, L5: 1). Architecture: 0-SEUL but DEEP TREE (L2/L1=1.42, up to L5!).
  12 colors: AB(254), MB(165), W(111), KB(79), RL(63), LC(41), GG(24), ?(14), RM(12), BG(1), MG(1).
  15 clusters. 38% empty — very sparse, partially-filled register.
  Provenance: Leymebamba — same site as UR006 (dated June 1473)!
  Museum: Centro Mallqui, Leymebamba, Amazonas.
  DAMAGED — OKR (Spanish): 'Una interrupcion por descomposicion del cordon principal; descartamos una cantidad de pendientes por su fragilidad.' Primary cord decomposed, some pendants discarded.
  OKR: 'Parece que hay variacion en la direccion de anudar los nudos finales (unos en Z, otros en S). Yo no he anotado las direcciones.' S/Z direction NOT recorded.
  OKR: 'Por falta del tiempo...' Recording INCOMPLETE.
  22 STRING (95% hits — 21/22), 451 INT, 296 EMPTY.

HYPOTHESIS
  UR003 is an integrated agricultural calendar from Leymebamba. Reclassified from 'astronomical' to AGRICULTURAL CALENDAR after vocabulary re-analysis: 12/13 words make sense as farming vocabulary vs 5/13 in pure astronomical reading. In the Inca worldview, agriculture and astronomy were NOT separate: the Pleiades (kaki) signal when to plant corn (also kaki), dark constellations (qaqa) mark rainy seasons, and the ceque system coordinates temples, farming, and star-watching as ONE unified system.

TREE READING — KEY DISCOVERY
  ALL 21 STRING cords are on L2 or L3 — NONE on L1. The tree structure is INVERTED compared to judicial khipus:
    L1 = NUMERICAL entry (count, measurement, quantity) <- PRIMARY data
      L2 = TEXT LABEL (what is counted) <- annotations identifying the numbers
        L3 = SUB-LABEL (additional specification)
  The NUMBERS are primary. The WORDS are annotations. Hallmark of an AGRICULTURAL PRODUCTION REGISTER.
  5-level tree depth encoding:
    L1 = Production period (281 entries — all INT/EMPTY)
      L2 = Category LABEL (400 entries — 20 STRING: qaqa=terrain, kaki=corn)
        L3 = Detail (79 entries — 1 STRING: qaqa)
          L4 = Sub-detail (8 entries)
            L5 = Individual detail (1 entry)

KEY VOCABULARY
  qaqa (rocky terrain/terraces x7) — DOMINANT word, terrain labels for fields
  kaki (corn/Pleiades x2) — dried corn harvest AND planting signal
  waki (communal labor x2) — mit'a cooperation
  chiqa (field inspection/verification x2) — governance
  paka (stored harvest x1) — hidden crops
  taqa (field boundaries x1) — land management
  kaqa (terrace walls-AY x1) — agricultural infrastructure
  waqa (huaca x1) — sacred site / offerings
  maqa (harvesting x1) — striking the grain
  maki (manual labor x1) — fieldwork
  llaqa (village x1) — which community farms
  kiki (self x1) — individual mit'a worker
  qaki (frost/dry? x1) — unknown
  Key insight: In Inca worldview, kaki = 'corn' AND 'Pleiades' because the Pleiades TELL when to plant corn. qaqa = 'rocky terrain' AND 'dark constellations.' No separation between agricultural and astronomical calendars.

COLOR-AS-ROLE
  AB (254 cords, 9 STRING): GEOGRAPHIC — terrain labels (qaqa x6, kaqa, kaki, chiqa)
  LC (41 cords, 6 STRING): RITUAL/LABOR — ceremonial aspect of farm work (qaki, kiki, waki, maki, chiqa)
  GG (24 cords, 3 STRING): GOVERNANCE — boundaries, storage, corn tribute (paka, taqa, kaki)
  MB (165 cords, 1 STRING): HUACA — waqa (sacred site)
  KB (79 cords, 1 STRING): ECLIPSE/IMPACT — maqa (astronomical event marker)
  LC carries 6/21 words (29%) = highest ritual proportion of any khipu analyzed.
  Cross-color actors: qaqa crosses AB->LC (geographic->ritual), kaki crosses AB->GG (subject->governance), chiqa crosses LC->AB (ritual->subject).

CROSS-CORPUS — THE LEYMEBAMBA PAIR
  UR003 vs UR006 (both Leymebamba):
    UR006 = ASTRONOMICAL OBSERVATION JOURNAL (monthly detail, datable June 1473, celestial labels)
    UR003 = AGRICULTURAL CALENDAR (hierarchical, 5 levels, farming vocabulary)
  UR006 records WHAT the sky shows. UR003 records WHAT the community does in response.
  Together: observe the stars -> plan the agriculture -> assign the labor -> verify the results.
  Blas Valera connection: Leymebamba is in Chachapoyas. Valera (1544-1597), born in Chachapoyas, described the Pachaquipu (astronomical calendar khipu) matching UR006's structure.

KEY PASSAGES
  1. qaqa x7 — dominant word. More qaqa than any other Leymebamba khipu.
  2. chiqa x2 — not in UR006. Suggests VERIFICATION entries.
  3. waqa x1 — huaca reference. Possibly the observatory huaca.
  4. llaqa x1 — village. Connects astronomy to a specific community.
  5. LC color = 41 cords (5%) — ritual color prominent. Astronomical observation was RITUAL, not purely scientific.
  6. Tree depth L5 — most data-rich structure in corpus.

NARRATIVE
  At Leymebamba, in the cloud forests of Chachapoyas, two khipus managed the relationship between sky and soil. UR006, the astronomical journal, recorded what the observers saw each month — the Pleiades rising, Mars in opposition, eclipses darkening the moon. UR003, the agricultural calendar, translated those observations into action: which village (llaqa) would work which fields, where the terraces (qaqa, kaqa) were, when the corn (kaki) would be planted, how the communal labor (waki) would be organized.
  The calendar descends five levels deep — from the production period at the top, through category labels, details, sub-details, to a single individual task at the bottom. The numbers come first; the words explain what was counted. This is a farmer's register, not a philosopher's notebook. But the ritual color LC pervades: 29% of all text labels are marked as ceremonial. In the Inca world, planting corn was as sacred as watching the stars. The same word — kaki — meant both.
references: Sivan 2026, ALBA Project — Agricultural calendar analysis, reclassification from astronomical
OKR field notes (Spanish) documenting damage and incomplete recording
Blas Valera (1544-1597). Pachaquipu description matching Leymebamba astronomical system
Inca agricultural-astronomical integration: Urton 1981, At the Crossroads of the Earth and the Sky
